evals.prompt: You are a Builder agent. Read the spec at the provided path and create BDD scenarios, a state machine, a glossary, and a coverage map. Write all output files to the specified output directory.
evals.expected_output: 4 artifact files with BDD scenarios in Gherkin format, state transitions, glossary, and coverage map

assertions.text: Output contains at least 3 BDD scenarios with Given/When/Then format
Each scenario has a confidence tag: SOLID, INFERRED, or GAP
A state machine or state transition section lists at least 2 states (Open, Done) with transitions between them
A glossary section defines at least 2 domain terms
A coverage map lists feature areas with scenario counts
No features are invented that are not in the spec or reasonably implied by it

evals.prompt: You are a Builder agent. Read the EDI specification excerpt and create BDD scenarios, a state machine, a glossary, and a coverage map. This is a complex enterprise document workflow system.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive artifacts covering document lifecycle, approval, execution, and notifications

assertions.text: Output contains at least 15 BDD scenarios covering document lifecycle, approval, and execution
State machine lists at least 5 states from the spec (Draft, On Registration, Registered, On Approval, Approved, On Execution, Executed, Archived)
At least 3 scenarios are tagged GAP or INFERRED, indicating areas where the spec is silent
Coverage map identifies at least one area with zero or low coverage
References to external systems (e.g., registration number source) are tagged as GAP, not assumed

evals.prompt: You are a Validator agent. You receive the original spec and Builder artifacts. You have NOT seen how these artifacts were created. Attack them. Find dead-end states, open questions, role conflicts, timing collisions, and missing functional areas.
evals.expected_output: Findings including dead-end states and open questions about the Approved state timeout

assertions.text: At least 1 dead-end state identified (the Approved state with no resolution timeout is a known dead-end)
At least 1 open question about what happens when a document stays in Approved state indefinitely
Each open question has proposed options (A/B/C) with trade-offs
Each finding has a severity rating (BLOCKER, HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW)
Findings do NOT reference Builder reasoning or how scenarios were created
At least one finding about missing functional areas (search, reporting, or archiving)

evals.prompt: You are a Validator agent. You receive the original spec and Builder artifacts for a simple todo application. Attack them. Find any issues.
evals.expected_output: Few or no significant findings for this clean, simple spec

assertions.text: No BLOCKER findings (the todo spec is simple and complete for its scope)
Total findings count is less than 8 (some LOW/MEDIUM findings are acceptable)
If zero findings reported, a note acknowledges this is unusual and warrants review (cognitive rule #4)

evals.expected_output: Full report.md and summary.json with findings, dead-ends, open questions, and BDD scenarios

assertions.text: report.md contains Executive Summary section
report.md contains a Verdict that is one of: READY, NEEDS_WORK, NOT_READY
At least 1 dead-end state identified in the report
At least 3 open questions with severity ratings and proposed options
Coverage map identifies at least 1 functional area with zero coverage
summary.json is valid JSON containing keys: scenarios_total, open_questions, dead_ends, verdict
Round artifacts exist: rounds/round-1/ and rounds/round-2/ directories with Builder and Validator files

evals.expected_output: Same report structure as fast mode, but produced via isolated subagents

assertions.text: report.md contains all required sections: Executive Summary, Verdict, Findings, Dead-Ends, Open Questions, Scenarios, Coverage
Validator findings do NOT contain phrases referencing Builder reasoning (e.g., 'the builder chose', 'builder decided', 'as built by')
summary.json is valid JSON with all required keys
At least 1 dead-end state identified
At least 3 open questions with severity ratings

evals.prompt: /builder-validator "Online store: users browse products, add to cart, checkout with credit card or PayPal. Admin manages inventory and sees orders. Need email notifications for order status changes."
evals.expected_output: BDD scenarios generated from free-form idea, with many GAP-tagged scenarios and open questions about missing areas

assertions.text: At least 5 BDD scenarios generated from the free-form input
Majority of scenarios tagged INFERRED or GAP (input is not a formal spec)
At least 3 open questions about missing areas (e.g., returns/refunds, shipping, tax, user registration, payment failure handling)
Report generated with all required sections despite informal input
Report body is written in Russian, not English
BDD scenarios use Russian language for descriptions
Verdict field uses English keyword (READY, NEEDS_WORK, or NOT_READY)
summary.json keys are in English, values in Russian where applicable

evals.expected_output: Report with banking-specific findings enhanced by domain patterns

assertions.text: Findings reference at least one banking-specific pattern (e.g., transaction limits, approval authority matrix, sanctions screening edge case)
At least 1 finding about concurrent or timing-related issues (e.g., two approvals at same time, limit check race condition)
Report contains findings beyond what generic analysis would produce
